title: Auxins for improved plant growth and branching
abstract: Plant branching can be increased by the application of an auxin to the plant. The auxin is a glucosamine including D-glucosamine, as well as salts thereof. Glucosamine application can be in the form of a powder or a solution applied to the roots, stems or leaves of a plant or other means.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  I claim: 1. A method of increasing branching in a plant comprising applying an auxin to the plant wherein said auxin comprises a glucosamine or salts thereof. 2. The method according to claim 1 wherein said glucosamine consists essentially of D- glucosamine, glucosamine sulfate glucosamine hydrochloride or blends thereof. 3. The method according to claim 2 wherein said glucosamine activates otherwise dormant meristematic cells causing axillary buds to actively grow one or more shoots or leaves. 4. The method according to claim 3 wherein said plant has strong apical dominance that prevents the axillary buds from actively growing in the absence of the application of glucosamine. 5. The method according to claim 3 wherein said plant increases in size by at least about 100% 6. The method according to claim 5 wherein said axillary buds develop vegetative shoots. 7. The method according to claim 5 wherein said axillary buds develop reproductive shoots. 8. The method according to claim 5 wherein said glucosamine is in the form of a powder. 9. The method according to claim 5 wherein said glucosamine is in an aqueous solution. 10. The method according to claim 5 wherein said glucosamine is applied to the roots of said plant 11. The method according to claim 5 wherein said glucosamine is applied to the stem or one or more side shoots off of said stem. 12. The method according to claim 5 wherein said glucosamine is applied to one or more leaves of said plant. 13. The method according to claim 3 wherein said glucosamine is applied to the roots by means of one or more time-release capsules. 14. The method according to claim 3 wherein said activation of dormant meristematic cells causes axillary buds to actively grow one or more shoots or leaves in racemose branching. 15. The method according to claim 3 wherein said activation of dormant meristematic cells causes axillary buds to actively grow one or more shoots or leaves in uniparous cymose branching. 16. The method according to claim 15 wherein said uniparous cymose branching is scorpoid cyme branching on successive branches. 17. The method according to clam 3 wherein said activation of dormant meristematic cells causes axillary buds to actively grow one or more shoots or leaves in helicoid cyme branching. 18. The method according to claim 15 wherein said branching is helicoid cyme branching on successive branches. 19. The method according to claim 3 wherein said branching is dichasium branching. 20. The method according to claim 3 wherein said branching is trichasial branching.
